key_insight: Bulk dealloying can occur far below the classical parting limit because diffusion-induced recrystallization creates dense grain-boundary networks that enable molten-salt infiltration and selective dissolution.
engineering_relevance: This mechanism helps predict and control corrosion or porous-structure formation in high-temperature electrochemical environments relevant to nuclear, aerospace, and energy materials.
method: Crystallographic and elemental mapping of grain-boundary/void architecture
Correlation of microstructure with anomalous bulk dealloying morphology
evidence_signal: The abstract reports direct 3D mapping of grain-boundary void structures and correlated elemental/crystallographic analyses showing a discontinuous-precipitation-like morphology below the usual parting limit.
watch_item: The mechanism is inferred from microstructural correlation, so its applicability may depend strongly on alloy chemistry, temperature, and molten-salt environment.
paper_title: Anomalous bulk dealloying below the parting limit
